channel.title: Research on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Research on Alien Truth Social

item.title: British UFO Research Association
item.description:
The British UFO Research Association (BUFORA) is a UK organization dedicated to investigating UFO phenomena in the British Isles. Founded in 1964, it reportedly had around 1,000 members in 1997 and investigates over 400 cases annually, with 95% identified as hoaxes. BUFORA has held annual conferences since 1987 and supports witnesses who believe they have encountered extraterrestrials. The organization has also been critical of the UK government’s handling of UFO-related information under the Freedom of Information Act.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_UFO_Research_Association

item.title: National UFO Reporting Center
item.description:
The National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC) is a nonprofit corporation in Washington State that documents UFO and UAP sightings. Founded in 1974, it has cataloged nearly 170,000 reported sightings, primarily in the United States. The center provides statistics, graphs, and maps to aid in research and offers a 24-hour hotline and online form for submitting reports. Peter Davenport has served as director since 1994, and the organization is largely funded by Davenport and Christian Stepien.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_UFO_Reporting_Center

item.title: National Investigations Committee On Aerial Phenomena
item.description:
The National Investigations Committee On Aerial Phenomena (NICAP) was a UFO research organization active in the United States from 1956 to 1980. Despite facing financial difficulties, NICAP gained significant visibility and respectability, advocating for scientific investigation of UFO sightings and arguing for a governmental cover-up of UFO evidence. The organization’s membership fluctuated, peaking in the early 1960s before declining sharply after the publication of the Condon Report. NICAP officially dissolved in 1980, and its archives were later acquired by the Center for UFO Studies.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Investigations_Committee_On_Aerial_Phenomena

item.title: National UFO Conference
item.description:
The National UFO Conference (NUFOC) was an annual conference based in San Diego, California, with a mission to present research in ufology. It began in 1964 as the Congress of Scientific Ufologists and was the longest active conference in the United States for presenting information on the UFO phenomenon. In 2004, James W. Moseley retired as Permanent Chairman, and Lisa Davis took over as Executive Director, aiming to increase public awareness of UFO phenomena by expanding contacts within the entertainment community. Davis, active in ufology since 1997, also founded The Foundation For Abductee Research and Support.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_UFO_Conference

item.title: CEFORA: Argentinean UFO Phenomenon Studies Committee
item.description:
CEFORA, the Argentinean Republic Committee for UFO Phenomena Studies, is an organization formed by various ufology groups in Argentina. Its primary objective is the declassification of all UFO-related phenomena in Argentina. CEFORA has formally requested information from the National Ministry of Defense, the National Presidency, and the Chamber of Deputies. Silvia Pérez Simondini serves as the director of CEFORA and also runs the Visión OVNI UFO Museum in Victoria, Entre Ríos.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cefora
